channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Board game convention set to return to Quezon City in May 2026
item.description: A popular board game convention is set to make its return to Quezon City this coming May, bringing together enthusiasts, collectors, and casual players for a shared celebration of tabletop gaming cult...

item.title: Armed hijacking targets City Power contractors working on substations in Alexandra
item.description: Two City Power contractors were violently hijacked while carrying out essential maintenance work on mini substations in Alexandra, Gauteng, as part of efforts to protect critical electricity infrastru...

item.title: Milwaukee Bucks Dismiss Doc Rivers Following Disappointing Season and Playoff Failure
item.description: The Milwaukee Bucks have parted ways with head coach Doc Rivers after a disappointing 2025–26 NBA season that ended with a 32-50 record and no playoff berth. Rivers, who joined the Bucks midway throug...

item.title: New Research Shows Significant Non-Compliance with Australia's Under-16 Social Media Ban
item.description: Australia implemented a pioneering ban on social media accounts for individuals under 16 years old in late 2025, aiming to protect young people from online harms such as cyberbullying, addiction, and ...

item.title: Trump criticises Pope Leo XIV as 'weak on crime' and 'terrible for foreign policy' over Iran war and immigration stances
item.description: United States President Donald Trump has launched a strong verbal attack on Pope Leo XIV, the first American pontiff, following the Pope's public criticism of US policies. In a Truth Social post, Trum...

item.title: Diplomatic talks: Managing expectations is key
item.description: When US Vice President JD Vance and Iranian Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf visited Islamabad, the Pakistani public was already expecting a major breakthrough. Media coverage, billboards, and social ...

item.title: Childhood myths and lessons from parental stories
item.description: In this article, Caroline Njunge reflects on the common myths and lies that parents in Kenya used to tell their children, often to enforce discipline or instill good manners. She shares personal exper...

item.title: Daily Horoscopes and Guidance for April 13, 2026
item.description: The Globe and Mail’s daily horoscope for April 13, 2026, offers astrological guidance for each zodiac sign, highlighting personal and social dynamics. Those celebrating a birthday today can benefit fr...

item.title: Diesel price surge grounds over 80% of fishing vessels in Nigeria
item.description: Na serious matter dey happen for the fishing industry as rising diesel prices don ground more than 80 percent of vessels wey belong to Nigerian trawlers. According to members of the Nigerian Trawlers ...

item.title: Kolkata Voters Express Cautious Views Ahead of West Bengal Elections 2026
item.description: As West Bengal gears up for the assembly elections on April 23 and 29, Kolkata presents a unique political atmosphere. Unlike previous elections marked by loud political debates, current discussions a...

item.title: Kenya Police Arrest 10 Suspects in Makadara Gang Crackdown
item.description: Kenya Police have successfully dismantled a criminal gang operating in Nairobi's Makadara estate, arresting ten suspects in a well-coordinated operation on Saturday. Officers from South B Police Stati...

item.title: Former NFL player Chris Payton-Jones dies aged 30 in Florida road accident
item.description: Former American football player Chris Payton-Jones, aged 30, has tragically died following a head-on collision in Florida. Payton-Jones, who once played as a cornerback for the University of Nebraska ...

item.title: Entrepreneurship Education as a Pathway to Economic Freedom for South African Youth
item.description: Freedom Day in South Africa commemorates the historic democratic elections of April 27, 1994, symbolising political freedom and the end of apartheid. However, in 2026, Dr Steven Zwane highlights that ...

item.title: Chandra Bose joins TMC, criticises BJP policies ahead of West Bengal polls
item.description: On Sunday, Chandra Bose, the grandnephew of Subhas Chandra Bose, formally joined the Trinamool Congress (TMC) in Kolkata, in the presence of state education minister Bratya Basu and TMC MP Kirti Azad....

item.title: FG’s tariff cuts on drugs, rice, and cars draw mixed reactions from key sectors
item.description: The Federal Government’s 2026 fiscal policy has triggered mixed reactions after it reduced import duties on pharmaceutical products, rice, cars and other items. In the health sector, pharmacists welco...

item.title: Quebec prepares for leadership transition as François Legault steps down
item.description: Quebec is entering a period of political transition following the announcement that Premier François Legault will step down from his role. The province's citizens are now awaiting the selection of his...

item.title: Tips on Caring for Someone's Heart Shared by ‘CarTon’ on It’s Showtime
item.description: ABS-CBN’s It’s Showtime recently featured a segment called ‘CarTon’ that tackled the delicate topic of how to care for someone’s heart, both emotionally and mentally. The segment explored practical ad...

item.title: Britney Spears enters rehab to address substance abuse after DUI incident
item.description: Britney Spears, the 44-year-old Grammy-winning pop star, has voluntarily entered a rehabilitation facility to address long-standing substance abuse issues, following her DUI arrest in Ventura County, ...

item.title: Hungarian Election Sees Orban Concede Defeat
item.description: In a surprising turn of events, Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán conceded defeat in Sunday's general election (Monday AEST), marking a significant shift in the nation's political landscape and po...

item.title: Police Operation in Islamabad Leads to Injuries and Arrests Among Afghan Residents
item.description: In Islamabad, a police operation targeting Afghan residents in the Sarai Kharboza area escalated into a violent clash, leaving four police officials injured. According to the FIR filed at Golra Police...
